entry.title: 2025 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Korea Partner Enablement
entry.content:
We are pleased to invite you to an online session introducing the new 2502 & 2504 DRC reports (Manufacturing Cost Statement & Simplified Withholding tax payment Statement for Resident Business/Other Income), scheduled for March 31st at 4:00 PM (Korean time).

Agenda & Speakers:
🕓4:00 PM – 5:00 PM: Manufacturing Cost Statement (Speaker: Kim, Charley, Consultant, SAP)
🕔5:00 PM – 6:00 PM: Simplified Withholding Tax Payment Statement for Resident Business/Other Income (Speaker: Kim-Nitsch, Me Young, Local Product Manager, SAP)

🔹Target Audience: Finance (Tax) Consultants
🔹Fee: Free of charge
🔹This session will be conducted in Korean.

Registration link

If you want to get more information about the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance in South Korea provided by SAP experts, join the South Korea - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Work Zone group.

entry.title: Re: Error importing Business Content: Integrated Financia...
entry.content:
@carsten4711, the error message
Couldn't import object 'SAP_FI_IFP_IM_Profitability [C464JF9W96BB9FKSL4UKOI3L6O]' : Parent resource does not exist
indicates that at least one of the folders is missing.

You mentioned that you deleted folders, could it be that you also deleted the Public folder?
All other folders are available in the package and should be recreated during the import.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: 500 Failed to find credentials for service com.sap.spa.processautomation for request
entry.content: Hi Ashutosh,

entry.title: Re: 500 Failed to find credentials for service com.sap.spa.processautomation for request
entry.content:
Hi @ashutoshrambhal,
I am facing a similar situation, could you please elaborate more on your answer?

entry.title: DRTM Compensation and Variable Pay Base Date
entry.content:
Dear Everyone,

Reaching out for help/assistance/guidance on this matter.
Business wants this requirement where Base Date of Retention time for DRTM Compensation/Variable Pay is differ from the standard Base Date. As you may know, the base date is the date from which retention times are calculated. For instance, if the Compensation/VP personal statement was created on 1st of May 2025 and configure retention time is 5 years then retention time for the statement would be five years after its created

Standard:
Object: Worksheet; Base Date: Completion Date

Object: Statements; Base Date: Statement creation date

this company supports multiple countries. these are some dates they wanted to use as base date

  • Worksheet (Active Period) - must keep it for 5 years after reaching the applicable retirement age
  • Worksheet (Inactive Period) - 10 years after termination
  • Statements (Inactive Period) - 7 years post leave date (termination date)
  • For the rest (Active Period) - they wanted to keep it while active/ No deletion while active, so I configured the retention time as 999 (year). Please advise if that would be fine

Please let me know if you have any idea to achieve this requirement. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAPFM06P - BADI_MM_PO_OC_EMAIL for S4CORE 107 and SAP...
entry.content: Hi Raymond,

entry.title: Re: SAPFM06P - BADI_MM_PO_OC_EMAIL for S4CORE 107 and SAP...
entry.content: Hi Raymond, thanks a lot for the feedback. Unfortunately the descibed solutions only work for the new output management and/ or ADOBE and/ or BRF+, which we are all not using. The mentioned enhancement spots in SAPFM06P are also no longer available within our version. But thank you for your feedback. BR Lukas

we require to run this in background / batch job.

Any ideas / descriptions how-to?

Help appreciated! Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP CDP- Delete Activities from Customer Schema
entry.content: Thank you for the suggestion, Oleh! I will try as you suggested and let you know then. Best Regard - Htet Htet

entry.title: Useful way to control rules triggering when a candidate comes from Recruitment & Onboarding
entry.content:
If you are deploying Onboarding into an instance that is already live with Employee Central, then during the implementation will need to be mindful of the rules that have already been created.    If you have data created in recruitment and then mapped to Onboarding, then rules may disrupt the data by triggering again. 

For example, in Employee Central we have the following rule that opens recurring pay components and populates the data.  However, when data is sent from recruitment this data can be stripped out by the rule triggering again, especially for ‘oninit’ rules where you can’t control the context.

One alternative is to configure the solution below.

1. Create a picklist as follows.

2. Create a custom field on ‘employmentinfo’ as shown below and assign this to employee and onboardee person types.

3. In recruitment create a custom field with the picklist and using a rule set the value to RCM

4. Map this to the custom field to the field ‘employmentinfo’. 

5. Build the following rule and assign this ‘onchange’ to a field in job information.  This will set the value = ONB if you manually start onboarding.

6. Set the context as follows.

7. Create this rule.

8. Assign this rule on change of a field in job information with the following context.  This will set the value = EC when you manually add a hire in Employee Central.

This will now ensure the custom field in employment info is populated as follows:

  • Sent from Recruitment = RCM
  • Manually started in Onboarding = ONB
  • Hired directly into Employee Central = EC

You can now use the custom field to trigger rules to behave differently based on how you start the process.

Our original rule now looks like this:

This field is very useful in allowing other rules to behave different especially for ‘oninit’ rules when the rules context is not supported.

entry.title: Re: Consulta Multiplos y Cantidades Mínimas de Pedidos de Compra por Proveedor
entry.content: Manuel/Sandra , esto se puede hacer en ERP o hana?

entry.title: Re: Local renaming of an IBP attribute within a MS Excel...
entry.content: Hi Peter, I don't see the "Attributes" option (running 2502 add-in) in the Members Names dropdown. Is it possible that a custom attribute / custom description is named "Attributes" in your PA ?

entry.title: Re: Join our Espresso Session on SAP's Utilities Strategy and the Upcoming Conference
entry.content: Looking forward to the session 🙂

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content:
Yeees! Thank you, Junwu!

I've replaced in code a token fetching with:

token_response = requests.post(
    token_url,
    data={
        "grant_type": "password",
        "username": username,
        "password": password,
        "client_id": client_id,
        "client_secret": client_secret
    },
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"}
)

and I'm receiving now:

{
  "@odata.context": "$metadata",
  "value": [
    {
      "name": "ZC_TSENSORDAT_GIV",
      "url": "ZC_TSENSORDAT_GIV"
    }
  ]
}

 However, I'm trying to get the data from the API, I'm getting error. But this error issues even in the browser. But it is different story...

{
  "error": {
    "code": "/IWCOR/CX_OD_NOT_FOUND/005056A509B11ED199D8826D151F80FE",
    "message": "The server has not found any resource matching the Data Services Request URI",
    "@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory": "Client_Error",
    "innererror": {
      "ErrorDetails": {

      }
    }
  }
}

 Anyway, thank you so much for your help, Junwu!

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 5: SAP Customer Experience, SAP Industry Cloud - Retail
entry.content:
This fifth post in my series gives an overview of latest UX innovations in the cloud in SAP Customer Experience and SAP Industry Cloud – Retail, to help your teams in sales and support. For SAP Customer Experience, these are the new Intelligent Sales Order in SAP Sales Cloud, and the Enterprise Service Management Business Document in SAP Service Cloud. For Retail, it is the new SAP Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit application.

 

SAP Customer Experience

SAP Sales Cloud - Intelligent Sales Order

The Intelligent Sales Order in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 revolutionizes the order-taking process with a seamless, intuitive interface designed for sales professionals.

With real-time synchronization to SAP ERP, users can effortlessly create and manage orders while ensuring accurate pricing and inventory updates. The intuitive design enables quick, error-free order entry, with automatic draft saving that allows sales reps to refine details before finalizing.

AI-powered price simulation provides intelligent recommendations, helping users assess pricing scenarios and optimize deals with confidence. The cloud-based, mobile-friendly infrastructure ensures seamless access from anywhere, empowering sales teams to work efficiently on the go.

By prioritizing usability and automation, SAP Sales Cloud V2 enhances productivity while delivering an optimized, frictionless user experience. Overall, it gives users these benefits:

  1. Seamless Order Creation:
    • The system enables quick order entry with an intuitive UI.
    • Easily select products, apply discounts, and manage pricing with ease.

  2. Integration with SAP Ecosystem:
    • Seamless synchronization with SAP ERP, ensuring accurate inventory and pricing.
    • Real-time data updates enhance decision-making and reduce errors.

  3. Enhanced User Experience:
    • A modern interface designed for sales reps to complete tasks faster.
    • Guided workflows simplify processes and improve efficiency.

  4. Mobile & Cloud Benefits:
    • Access order management features anytime, anywhere.
    • Cloud-based infrastructure ensures scalability and security.

Figure 1 shows the whole sales order screen. To get a thorough look at its capabilities, watch this:

  • Video: Order Taking in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 in Action (28:33 min.)

Figure 1: Intelligent Sales Order in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2. ALT Text: The image shows the application with the tab “Order Summary” selected at the top. The left quarter of the screen has a dark blue side bar with overview information, such as Ship To party, contact etc, below that statistical information, order simulation summary and available to promise (ATP) information, viusalized in a chart showing how much is on time, delayed and blocked. The right three-quarters of the screen shows the total amoung above a list of 10 products in cart. Below that a section “Order Pricing” showing discount, profit margin, surcharge, quality check fee, followed by a section “Notes” with one note showing three bullet points and at the bottom a section “Involved Parties” showing two cards with photos of two contacts.

SAP Service Cloud – Enterprise Service Management Business Document

SAP ESM Business Document offers a smart solution for processing various document types, using AI to automate workflows and enhance accuracy. This solution makes document processing more streamlined and responsive, improving overall business workflow efficiency. Users benefit as follows:

  1. AI-Powered Data Extraction:
    • Leverages advanced AI to process emails and attachments, efficiently extracting key details without needing historical data or specific languages.

  2. Business Document Designer
    • Empowers administrators to customize tables, define fields, and set up actions
    • User-friendly interface simplifies setup and automations.

  3. Flexible Schema:
    • Customize data extraction and organization to fit specific business needs.

  4. Seamless Integration:
    • Easily integrates with systems like S/4HANA for data posting and result storage.

  5. Complete Process Automation:
    • Automates document handling and triggers actions through APIs.

Figure 2 shows how users get an overview of Business Documents and their status. Read more about this here:

  • Advanced Document Processing with SAP Enterprise Service Management.

Figure 2: Business Document extraction in SAP Service Cloud – Enterprise Service Management. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot showing a case. The left quarter in dark blue summarizing the case, showing customer name, changed on date, status (open) and priority (urgent), description, category and service level (i.e. vertical timeline showing steps and dates for “Case Reported”, “Initial Review”, “Initial Response”). The right three-quarters shows a four-step workflow at the top, with step 3 “Resolution” currently selected. Below that three buttons in a “Next Best Action” section: Connect with Product Expert, Check Invoice Status, and Schedule Phone Call with Supplier. Below that five tabs are offered: Timeline, General, Finance, Business Document (selected), History. Most of the screen is filled with a list of 12 Business Documents, showing source, with an AI icon and percentage (98%, 94%,…) next to those automatically extracted. The list has columns Status, Doc Type, No., Date..

 

SAP Industry Cloud - Retail

SAP Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit (OSTA)

For retailers it is very important to know which products are selling well, which are not, and also to have transparency about inventory at each store. This helps not only to ensure that stock doesn’t run out, it also helps them guide consumers to stores which have what they want if the store they visited does not stock it.

The newly developed SAP Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit application addresses these needs, with a modern design and intuitive user experience. As an example of one of the capabilities, Figure 3 shows what outbound monitoring looks like.

Figure 3: Outbound monitoring in SAP Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit (OSTA). ALT Text: Two screenshots are shown. On the left the transaction log, showing aggregatioin number, status and rule, with an example visible where one billing docuent contains two material document numbers with the information “updated manually” and some detail text . On the right a screenshot of the Data Transfer screen is shown in three rows of cards below a filter bar allowing users to filter on Aggregation Status, Number, Date, Rule and Store ID. Each row of cards reflects four-step workflow with steps “Ready”, “Sent”, Acknowledged”, and “Completed”. The cards have color indicators: green for done, yellow for in progress, red for error, and white for not yet started. A line above each row of cards contains a checkbox and information about the overall status, aggregation no., Store ID, date, and totals.

To find out more, have a look at this blog post and video:

  • SAP Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit a new sales transaction validation industry cloud solution.
  • Video: Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit (2:55 min.)

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this fifth part, covering SAP Customer Experience and SAP Industry Cloud - Retail.

I will post part six later this week, covering latest UX innovations for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More).

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori    
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: Re: How to Guide - Principal Propagation in an HTTPS Scenario

entry.title: Key User Custom Fields in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise: Dos and Don’ts
entry.content:
Teaser

Did you ever wonder how transporting key user custom fields works in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise and which apps you should use to avoid common mistakes? 
Do you want to know what to consider regarding key user custom fields when you upgrade your S/4HANA system? 

If your answer is yes, then this blog post is just for you. 

 

Disclaimer

Please note that this blog does exclusively address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise and does not address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
In this blog we use S/4HANA as an abbreviation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise. In addition, for key user custom fields we use the abbreviation custom fields. 

 

Table of Contents

This blog post covers the following topics: 

  • Dos and Don’ts in the context of software logistics
  • What to consider regarding custom fields in upgrade situations 
  • General Dos and Don’ts regarding custom fields 
  • Dos and Don’ts regarding ABAP managed fields (abbreviation AMF) 

 

Dos and Don’ts in the context of software logistics

When you work with key user custom fields in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and you come to the point where you want to transport your custom field then you work with so called “Software Collections”, which act like a safety box wrapping the technical transport process. 
This is different in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise. There you transport the custom field artifacts on your own using standard transport mechanism. With this you have more freedom, but there are many things that could go wrong. It can easily happen that you do not keep the managed and unmanaged objects of a custom field in sync, e.g. by forgetting to transport the unmanaged objects. 

Here is a list of Dos SAP recommends: 

  • Use Fiori apps for transport handling (also refer to note 2660797)
    • Register Extension for transport to
      • assign key user items to a package 
      • assign key user custom fields to a transport 
    • Configure Software Packages to  
      • configure automatic transport/task handling for packages 
      • assign transports to packages 
  • Register a so-called job user for generation processes (also refer to SAP Help Portal). You must ensure that: 
    • the job user is provided in maintenance view ATO_PK_JOB_USER (transaction SM30) 
    • the job user has sufficient authorization for publishing (e.g. SAP_ALL profile) 
    • the job user is valid (exists in correct client and is within validity range) 
    • operations (save/publish/delete) are performed in the correct client 

And here is a list of Don’ts: 

  • Never ever develop key user custom fields in several development systems – use one development system only 
    • Implemented checks are system specific --> no data of other systems are considered 
    • Generated artifact names depend on custom field name --> clash of object names may occur 
  • Never edit key user custom fields in several systems – only edit them in the one development system 
    • Custom field and its generated artifacts have a dedicated source system --> i.e. risk system inconsistency in case of custom field deletion, if generated objects exist only locally 

What to consider regarding custom fields in upgrade situations

There is one more crucial difference regarding lifecycle stability for custom fields when in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise compared to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the upgrade procedure contains a task where custom field artifacts are technically adapted on the fly, if needed due to changes in the SAP standard.  
This is different from the upgrade procedure in On-Premise or Private Cloud. In On-Premise and Private Cloud there is a key user custom field analysis process figuring out whether an adaptation is necessary. If this process identifies an adaptation need you will see the following messages in the upgrade log: 

Message message id 'CFD_REGENERATE_OP' message number '001' ('Regeneration of Custom fields necessary; refer to SAP Note 2640715') 

Please note that also the following relevant message could be shown: 

Message message id 'CFD_REGENERATE_OP' message number '016' ('Regeneration of datasource extension necessary; refer to SAP Note 2640715') 

This second message tells you that the issue is not an issue with a custom field but rather with a key user Datasource Extension object. You can find more details in note 2640715.  

Please note that it makes a difference whether you are upgrading the development system or e.g., a test or production system. 

In case you are upgrading the development system, and an adaptation need has been identified you should execute the following report: CFD_REGENERATE_IN_ONPREMISE. This report makes the needed adaptations. When you use it make sure that you are always on the newest note level of this report. 

In case you are upgrading a system different from the development system and an adaptation need has been identified you do not execute the report. Instead, you import the adaptations which have been made in the development system. 

There is one scenario where the situation is a bit more complicated. You may want to execute a test upgrade and to do so you copy the development system so that it gets a different system id. Or you must move your development system to another system so that the new development system has a different system id. 
In this case it matters that custom fields are only editable in their original source system. They are not editable in other systems. To enable their editability you have to follow note 3484189.  
BUT: only do this in the exceptional situations described above. Never ever maintain your key user custom fields in more than one system. Doing so will screw-up at least the custom fields. 

 

General Dos and Don’ts regarding custom fields 

There may be situations where you created a custom field with a certain name (Custom Field ID) and accidentally deleted it and now want to re-create it with exactly that same name. 
How that can be done differs from the context you are in: 

  • If the transport with the field creation has not been released, you can delete and recreate the field…even when choosing an incompatible field type 
  • As soon as the transport with the field creation has been released you can delete and recreate the field only as long as the field type chosen is compatible (see also note 3328541) 

Also, it may happen that you want to expose your custom field in an OData service (e.g. to make the field visible in a UI, which gets its data from this OData service) and this OData service is not offered to be enabled in the key user Custom Fields app.  
In this case SAP standard application development missed to enable the respective OData service for key user custom fields. In such a case you can create a respective extensibility enabling request via the customer influence channel. 
Please note that utilizing an OData redefinition approach in order to add key user custom fields to OData Service Entities does not work and is not supported (Note 1574568 constraint 34). 

 

Dos and Don’ts in the context of ABAP Managed Fields 

In case you utilize the feature ABAP Managed Fields (described based on a concrete example in the blog post Convert Manually Created Append Fields to Real Key User Custom Fields) which allows you to convert your manually created "old" append fields into real key user custom fields the Dos and Don’ts specific to this feature are relevant for you.  
The specific challenges regarding software logistics come from the fact that for ABAP Managed Fields, some of the custom field's artefacts are generated and managed by the Custom Fields framework, and some are customer-managed (e.g., the CDS Code View, CDS Text View, data element, append, …). As these customer-managed artifacts are not safe-guarded and controlled by the Custom Fields framework, one could easily break the custom field by changing these manually created objects in an incompatible way, or even accidentally delete them.  

Are ABAP Managed Fields lifecycle stable, and do they follow the clean core principles?

The general answer is yes but let us have a closer look at the details.  
As already explained, an ABAP Managed Field comes with a set of unmanaged objects: 

  • Data element and domain (manually created or SAP standard data element) 
  • Manually created append with the field type using the data element above 
  • Depending on the chosen semantic type, also a manually created CDS Code View and CDS Text View  

If all of these unmanaged objects are built with ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development, they automatically apply to the clean core rules. The ABAP Managed Field is 100% clean core compliant and fully lifecycle stable. 

In general, within your unmanaged objects, you may want to use non-C1 released objects. With that, you cannot fully follow the clean core path any longer. 

Let us dive a little bit deeper into the unmanaged objects to better understand how critical certain things are in terms of upgrade stability: 

Data element and domain

If the data element or domain you use to type your ABAP Managed Field is not an SAP standard object which is Released for Use in Cloud Development,, then this data element or domain could be changed incompatibly or even deleted with any updated version of S/4HANA. However, in a lot of cases it’s unlikely that this will happen because, for example, the data element is prominently used within the SAP standard (which means that it is used for the typing of key fields.) 

Append field

In some cases, the Extension Include Structure to which you add the field using an ABAP dictionary append is released for Extensibility in Cloud Development. Then, you can add this append in ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development. In case the Extension Include Structure is not released for developers but only for key users, you cannot add the DDIC append in ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development but only in Standard ABAP. This no longer completely follows the clean core principle, but can still be 100% upgrade-stable, as it’s protected with the C0-contract release for key users. Using ABAP language version “Standard”, there are no more lifecycle stability checks in place to prevent you from leaving the upgrade-stable way. Consider the following aspects to be 100% upgrade-stable: 

  • Make sure that you use ZZ as prefix of the field name, or your own customer namespace to avoid collisions with future standard fields or your own custom fields 
  • Make sure that you’re using the field suffix as defined in the respective business context in transaction SCFD_REGISTRY

CDS Code View and CDS Text View

In case your CDS view references SAP standard objects which are not Released for Use in Cloud Development (CDS views, data elements, database tables, …) then also these objects may be changed incompatibly or even deleted with an updated version of S/4HANA. In this case, 100% upgrade stability is no longer guaranteed. 

In the worst case, if one of the not “Released for Developers” (C1 released) SAP standard objects you reference has been changed incompatibly or has even been deleted, it can cause issues during a system upgrade. You’d have to rework your affected unmanaged objects to solve the issues. 

You can get more information regarding ABAP Managed Fields following these links:  

  • General handling: SAP note 2981484 
  • Enable ABAP managed fields for database tables with proxy views: SAP note 2463257 
  • Re-creating a custom field: SAP note 3327309 

Golden rules when working with ABAP Managed Fields

  • Use the ATO apps for transport handling (also refer to note 2660797) 
  • Only use these ABAP Managed Fields in the exceptional situations described above 
  • Use a suitable prefix for the append field (ZZ or real namespace) 
  • Use a suitable suffix for the append field depending on the business context 
  • Ensure that these objects are kept in the same software component as the append structure with the database field itself 
  • Ensure that the append field also exists in subsequent systems. Either the append field is imported before or transported together with the custom field 
  • Never delete append fields via SE11 before disabling the custom field via SCFD_EUI 
  • Never modify or delete the value help view used in AMF field of type List 
  • Never delete the data element used in AMF field 
  • Never continue with the publishing or deletion of further custom fields if a previous publishing or deletion failed 

Now, you are done! Congratulations! With this, you are now equipped to 

  • handle software logistics for custom fields 
  • avoid common pitfalls in this context. 

Please note that this blog has been elaborated by a team of experts, and I want to 
thank Yasmina van Rooij (YasminavanRooij), Daniel Wachs (DanielWachs), and Georg Wilhelm (georg_wilhelm) for their extremely helpful contributions.

 

Feedback, Comments, or Questions

You’re cordially invited to provide feedback, comments, or questions in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics. 
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/). 

Other related blog posts: 

  1. How to implement a custom field of type “Code List based on CDS view” with a developer extensibility custom entity 
    How to implement a custom entity in the ABAP RESTful Programming Model using remote function modules    
  2. How to configure the value help as a context-dependent value help 
  3. How to configure intent-based navigation on a key user custom field of type “Code List based on CDS View”    
    and in this case, how to easily find the fitting semantic object and semantic object parameter: Custom field type “Code list based on CDS view”: Finding/defining the right value help view and the right Semantic Object and Semantic Object Parameter for Intent-Based Navigation 
  4. Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility 
  5. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...) 
  6. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties 
  7. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding 
  8. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility 
  9. SAP Screen Personas: How to position key user custom fields exactly where you want them to be 
  10. Convert Manually Created Append Fields to Real Key User Custom Fields

entry.title: Re: Error importing Business Content: Integrated Financia...
entry.content: The folders i deleted were correct but after deletion i have to also delete them from the "Deleted files" folder - now it works. Thanks for your quick help!

entry.title: Re: Reimagining digital learning as an integrated part of a Digital Adoption Platform with SAP &&&&
entry.content: Hello Kristina, thank you very much for all this update. Does this mean that all clients who have SAP Enable Now need to migrate to Walkme?

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: more bypass options and custom extensions
entry.content:
Dear Alex,

We have been implementing the pipeline concept with bypass options for our P2P scenario, which involves only a single receiver. Following the guidelines outlined in the blog, we configured the setup accordingly. Since this is a straightforward single-receiver scenario, we did not upload the XSLT in the partner directory; instead, we simply maintained the string parameters.

The message successfully processed through all steps, completing without errors, as shown in the screenshot. However, we noticed that Step 1 of the standard pipeline remains in a processing status and does not complete, despite successfully writing messages to the inbound JMS queue (PIPQ01), from which Step 2 picks up and processes further.

Could you provide insights into why Step 1 is not completing? What could be causing this behavior?

entry.title: Re: Dimension Combination Rule in SAC
entry.content: Pl share if any update on showing only valid combination for the users

entry.title: Re: Restriction of Timesheet changes after approval from...
entry.content: Hello Seema, Like you suggested i have implemented the above BADI. But still employees are able to change the timesheets. And even in the trace the details of project manager is missing in order to apply the logic. Kindly help. Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to validate duplicate field values for custom MDF...
entry.content:
Hi,

I am not sure of the business use case why the value pf a field across two different object need to be unique.

This can be achieved like below but will result in performance issue as the data volume for given object increases over a period of time.

Here cust_of is a the object cust_string is a field which must be unique

entry.title: Intelligent serive centre is running multiple time while sync from job req to position
entry.content: Intelligent serive centre is running multiple time while sync from job req to position

entry.title: SUM DMO with S/4HANA conversion
entry.content:
Hi,

In an upcoming project we have to bring a HCM system which is currently running on Windows and MSSQL to Linux and HANA. In addition to that the system should also be converted from ECC to S/4HANA. I looked up some information on DMO and only found a help article where this option is mentioned in connection with hyperscalers.

Does anybody have experience with this scenario and tell me if DMO with all those requirements is possible to do in an on-premise landscape?

 

Best regards,

Alex

entry.title: The kanban registered as a bulk is not working (consuming the transfer. stock automat.). What to do?
entry.content:
We have kanbans that are created through the transactions PK01 and LPK1. And we edit it with PK02 and LPK2 if needed. The point is that we have some kanbans registered as a bulk (that consume the materials transfered for the production lines automatically), but this automation is not working.

For that it may works, we have a right Storage Type for the "normal" kanbans that is PS1 and a Storage Type for the bulk ones that is 160. When the material will be a bulk kanban, we insert this Storage Type on PK01 and LPK1. We also insert the information ZS65 in the Stock Transfer field on LPK1, and additionally the Cost Center from where it will be consumed in the tab Flow Control, in the same LPK1. We also flag the field "Bulk Material" in the tab MRP2 in the MM02 transaction. After it, the bulk is supposed to work, but it is not working, I mean that its not consuming the whole stock when transfered for the kanban box position.

Am I missing some important info and configuration that makes a bulk kanban works? What is missing for that it may works? Help me guys please.

#bulk #kanban

entry.title: BW transfer data to 3rd party system Oracle/MSSQL with field value filled with space
entry.content:
Hi BW experts, 

This is Manish Kesharwani here, I am working on BW4HANA implementation. 

Thank you for reading my message. 

Currently we are facing one challenge. 

S/4 to BW to Oracle/MSSQL 

We are receiving data from S/4 to BW and then we are distributing data from BW to 3rd party systems through Open hub push dbconnect which are non SAP systems using Oracle or MSSQL database. 

Every field that has character data type and is not filled in S/4 when transferred to Oracle or MSSQL, include a space as a value. 

Our customers have raised it as an issue, that in S/4 for that field is blank, then in Oracle, that field contain space is not consistent. their concern is source value must match with target value, even in cases when there is no input of data in source. 

Please guide me, if you have come across similar situation or how can I overcome this situation. 

Happy to connect with you, if you would like further explanation on it. 

Thank you so much for your time. 

 

Regards

Manish Kesharwani

entry.title: Re: BOM recursive (message no. 29110)
entry.content: This was fantastically helpful, thank-you!

entry.title: Re: Seeking Recommendations: Learning Resources for Aspiring SAP FICO Consultant
entry.content:
Hi Omar,

Please ping me on whats app +919515220124

entry.title: MDK - how to expose data as simple table on a phone with pagination and search ?
entry.content:
hello MDK experts! 

I am struggling with a "basic" requirement for which I would appreciate some guidance.

Requirement: I have a entityset, odata based. offline enabled. and I would like to display the results on a mobile phone one line per entry with 5 columns. the entity set contains more than 1000 items. which users would like to browse through

Issues faced: 

- data table object: look & feel on the phone is perfect. I like the sticky first column. exactly what I am looking for... unfortunately, data paging is not supported on iOS for this object. If I set the limit to 100, I cannot scroll and browse the entire list. and if I set the limit to 5000 I get all the data, but the rendering time is close to 30sec. and the scrolling down experience is strange (list is partially freezing.. probably due to high volumes)

- grid table: the data paging is fully operational...  but on mobile phone, the columns are rendered on 1 single column.. which is defeating the purpose. I need 1 line with 5 columns 

- object extension collection : same behavior as the grid. 

other options investigated: 

- tried to catch onScroll event on the data table: not working.

- tried implementing single page with previous and next button : very bad user experience 

- tried to implement an extension rendering a view with nativescript: I could not find example to leverage. and it would mean I also have to implement all the features such as the search in custom. onPress actions etc ). I was able to render a simple view, by I could not use the clientAPI context and perform a .read from the custom class for instance. 

- tried to get the current "page" number (items currently being scrolled on the table) in order to issue a customEvent when end of the page is reached: did not succeed.

 

hence this message hoping to get some guidance from experts.

1/ are there any plans to have datapaging supported for datatable on iOS in upcoming releases?

2/ any recommended extension samples close to this requirement? which could help me build a custom view? 

3/ other design suggestions? 

thanks a lot. 

Damien 

nota: I am using MDK 24.11.2, on iOS, branded app.

entry.title: Re: UPDATES for Principal Propagation in an HTTPS Scenario
entry.content: Please check the documentation here.

entry.title: Re: Massload import of attachments within the learning hi...
entry.content: @RafaelHernandez is there a way to Report on those attachments? For compliance and regulatory reasons we need to be able to see who is missing an attached certification

entry.title: How to work with a physical Tracking ID in SAP DM
entry.content:
We have 2 production lines. The products produced in the first are consumed in the second. For the first line we have created SFC's. At the start of the production a tracking code is generated and the SFC is relabelled to this tracking code, which is a Dot matrix code. The code is engraved on the product. After production the SFC is finished successfully.

For the 2nd line we created new SFC's. Also here a tracking ID is generated and the SFC is relabelled. During this production, the equipment regularly checks with a camera the identification on a product which is the tracking code generated in the previous line. It is added as Data collection to the current SFC. We have 2 different tracking codes (SFC's). One of the consumed product, the second of the current SFC in work. At the end of the line the second tracking code is engraved in the product. 

The PLC keeps track of which tracking ID is read and to which current SFC in work it belongs. 

For a new process an existing product is put in the line. The PLC reads the tracking ID and needs to select the SFC which it belongs to in a queue. But these are NOT the same since the tracking ID read is a data collection under the SFC in work. 

Is there any way to log some kind of tracking number for a SFC and in a PPD select the SFC by physical tracking ID? I've tried the following options:

- Search by Data collection value, this is not possible. You can get the full data collection but need to specify a period. We do not know when the part was produced in the first line. 

- Packing unit, we can create a packing unit, add the SFC and work from there on, only packing units can be manually broken and is not a fool proof process. 

- Process lot, this works fine but the process lot ID can only be numeric. The tracking ID we're using also contains alphabetical characters

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: more bypass options and custom extensions
entry.content:
@aravindkumar_srinivasan this is a bug with the new handled by integration flow feature of the XI adapter, I have raised an incident ticket, a patch for the tenants will be provided soon

Alex

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content:
GET - works perfectly well!

But with POST, I'm getting

403 {"error":{"code":"/IWBEP/CM_V4H_RUN/043","message":"CSRF token is missing","@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory":"CSRF_Token_Missing"}}

entry.content: This doesn't work. UI5 version 1.120.18

entry.title: Re: Value Help select all option
entry.content: Is there a setting or configuration that can do this if I'm using Fiori elements?

entry.title: Re: Review Booklet Designer (Concept and Adaptability) - CE2408
entry.content:
Hi Takuhito,
you are right the Cost Center Review Booklet shows "Application Outdated", this is due to a missing registration, which we will fix in the next release. It doesn't have any impact on your Problem.
Just as background this Status is only relevant for custom booklets, where you can see whether your booklet was changed after publishing.
The reason why you can't copy the Cost Center Review Booklet is due to the fact, that it was not yet released for custom adaptations. This setting is set by the SAP developer.
As far as I know the colleagues plan to release Cost Center Review Booklet in CE2508 for adaptation.
Kind Regards Stefan

entry.title: "This message is from an external sender" creates folder in clients mail box
entry.content:
Hello All, we are triggering mails to user which mail domain is out side of organization.

Smart form is generating this PDF and using CL_BCS we are triggering mail to user.

 

 

The issue is whenever we trigger PDF from invoice VF02, user is getting PDF attachment inside the folder, which is not happening in our mail ids.

It is something like Outlook is creating secure folder for user since mail is coming from outside of his organization, but user doesn’t need this folder.

If anyone faced this issue please let me know the solution, it could be from SAP or Outlook mail box setting.

entry.title: Re: Cannot overwrite data definitions with annotation.xml in Fiori Elements
entry.content: Hi, I'm having the same issue, did you figure out a solution?

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud Using Data Import API
entry.content:
This blog post aims to bring some introduction about the usage of SAC Data Import APIs and how they can be leveraged to replicate data to Public Dimensions. It will also describe of one use case implemented recently to fulfill particular requirements without going with the standard communication scenario.

Data Import APIs can be leveraged to replicate transactional and master data to SAP Analytics Cloud.

The API includes 5 different services that can be used via API endpoints. These can be tested using an API Client or Platform such as Postman, Insomnia.

  • Models service
  • PublicDimensions service
  • CurrencyConversions service
  • Jobs service
  • Import service

For more information about the these 5 services please consult this document inHelp Portal.

Also available inSAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Use Case (Example): Replicate Enterprise Project individually to SAP Analytics Cloud after creation or change in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Purpose of Use Case:

Replicate Enterprise Projects based on creation (or change) action in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Keep in mind this is just an example originated from a real requirement but can be adapted, with the respective complexity, to any other object if it fulfills the necessary conditions and systems involved to satisfy the need to replicate an object on individual basis from S4 Public Cloud to SAC.

Ultimately, this is available for any other middleware or point-to-point consumption if the Sender system allows it.

An High-Level Diagram with the components involved is available below:

  • One SAP BTP Subaccount where Event Mesh and Integration Suite are subscribed with respective instances and applications. For more information how to setup the Event Mesh from SAP Integration Suite check the following document.
  • S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services is a nice-to-have in this example but logically for productive scenarios it shall be included in the overall architecture.

 

Image Caption: Technical Architecture Design

 

 

After understanding which components are used in this scenario, next level Diagram explains the data flow between each SAP component to leverage the Data Import API. Remember that it can be adapted to use one of the other 5 services although might require adjustments, especially in the interface in SAP Integration Suite.

Pre-Requisites:

  • SAP Event Mesh and SAP Integration Suite integrated
  • S/4HANA Public Cloud Integrated with SAP Integration Suite, using for example OAUTH Client Credentials
  • S/4HANA Public Cloud Integrated with SAP Event Mesh. Consult the following document in case more information is needed to perform this step.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud configured with one Public Dimension and with OAUTH Authentication configured to be consumed by SAP Integration Suite. For more information how to configure the authentication, consult this document.

 

Image Caption: Process Flow Architecture Diagram

 

 

As legend for the above diagram, explaining the data flow process:

Business User executes a create or change action on the respective business object. In this use case the Enterprise Project.

  1. Create or Change event in S/4HANA Public Cloud releases that outbound even message towards Event Mesh, previously configured;
  2. Event Mesh consumes and subsequently is consumed by SAP Integration Suite;
  3. Once the event is received, SAP Integration Suite extracts the Enterprise Project ID to then execute a GET Call based on the Enterprise Project ODATA API to receive the object data payload to deliver to SAP Analytics Cloud;
  4. SAP Integration Suite interface needs to transform the payload into the structure the Public Dimension expects to successfully replicate it. This has been done via message mapping element but can be accomplished with a script as well;
  5. Finally, the delivery of the message to the dataimport API requires first an authentication, using OAUTH mechanism for example to post the data into the respective PublicDimension which will be defined in the HTTP endpoint.
    1. At this final step, as described in the documentation, SAC demands the CSRF Token which needs to be retrieved first and sent as Header in the final step. This step is described in this document.

 

The following diagram presents a simple example of an interface executing all the steps mentioned above from SAP Integration Suite perspective.

 

Image Caption: The main integration flow with all the steps to accomplish a simple scenario without complex error handling mechanism.

 

Image Caption: Local Integration Process to Post the Payload into SAP Analytics Cloud.

One important caveat to be successful is to understand the Public Dimension data structure. All the necessary attributes, either mandatory or optional, need to be included in the message mapping. If not, the execution will fail.

This can be either assessed from SAC UI, going into the Public Dimension and check the properties.

Image Caption: SAP Analytics Cloud – Modeler – Public Dimension

 

Alternatively, via GET Call, using for example Postman, this information can also be retrieved using the Public Dimension metadata, using the following URL:

https://{host}/api/v1/dataimport/publicDimensions/{publicDimensionID}/metadata

This procedure is explained in this document. One might see that in the Sample Response, all attributes part of the Public Dimension are retrieved with their respective data types and key identification.

Image Caption: Public Dimension Metadata Response Sample

 

 In case of failed executions/messages, SAC also provides a mechanism to verify and understand the job failure. If you remember, I mentioned the dataimport API includes 5 services, being one of them for “Jobs”.

For example /jobs/{jobID}/invalidRows can be used to assess what failed during the data import into the respective Public Dimension.

There are additional examples available in SAP Business Accelerator HUB, such as S/4HANA Pricing Information to SAP Analytics Cloud.

Hope this particular scenario can be of any service for particular situations and requirements.

Happy testing and continuous learning. Share with us any interesting use cases you have implemented which can support others and reach out in case of any issues.

SAP Analytics Cloud #SAC Data Import API SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Re: eInvoice details on Invoice form
entry.content:
Hello,

To achieve the requirement of sending invoice forms with eInvoice details (like IRN number and QR code) via email to the customer immediately after the billing document is created, and ensure that the document status is updated to complete automatically, you can try these steps:

1. **e-Invoicing Integration:**
- Ensure that your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system is integrated with the GST Invoice Registration Portal (IRP). This integration should automatically generate eInvoice details (such as IRN number and QR code) when a billing document is created.

2. **Configuration of eDocument Cockpit:**
- Utilize the eDocument Cockpit to manage and track the statuses of eDocuments. This cockpit will pull notification messages from tax authorities and update the process statuses of eDocuments.

3. **Automate Output Determination:**
- Configure your SAP system's Output Management feature to automatically trigger the email output when a billing document is created.
- To configure this, use the SAP S/4HANA Output Control or NACE transaction in older systems to set up an output type that will send the invoice form as an email attachment.

4. **Form Template Customization:**
- Use a predefined billing document form template (like `SDBIL_CI_STANDARD_IN`) and enhance it to include eInvoice details such as the IRN and QR code.
- If necessary, extend the form template with custom fields to ensure all eInvoice details are included.

5. **Automatic Email Trigger:**
- Modify the output condition records or set a default email output to ensure the email with the invoice and eInvoice details is sent immediately upon creation.
- Use the SAP Communication Management Framework to define and manage communication channels for email notifications to customers.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: issue "Unexpected Error" with test run of SAP report RSIRPIRL
entry.content:
Hi,

We're getting the test run result "Unexpected error" after executing the SAP report RSIRPIRL with the following input entries:

 

Based on "SAP Note: 1773722", it says that such errors can be ignored as this entry is referring to be corrupted and there is no physical document assigned to this entry.

But how would we make sure that the report test run performed correctly? Why this report doesn't show test run results?

 

Regards

Reddy

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 rendering issue on Android-based barcode scanner devices
entry.content: What version of Zebra EB are you using?

entry.title: Re: graduation project : SAP Based Smart Job Tracking System Hello,
entry.content: Hi Zeno, Thanks for your reply! My project is not about ABAP background jobs (SM37); it’s more focused on helping employees manage their tasks and ensuring a balanced workload distribution within SAP. Just to clarify, this is not a surveillance system. The main goal is to create a platform where employees can plan their tasks, track their progress, and analyze their workload based on predefined KPIs. The system is designed to help distribute work more efficiently, not to monitor individuals in real time. I understand your point about data privacy. Since this system mainly deals with task-related data rather than personal tracking, do you think I should still check with the DPP Unit? Would there be any major concerns in this case? I appreciate your guidance! Best, Zeynep

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAP Cloud ALM for Integration Suite
entry.content: 3414369 - Not possible to create an endpoint for SAP Integration Suite service under LMS - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Annotation File Managere is not supported
entry.content: I've the very same issue. any updates?

entry.title: Re: Error M3897 raised with parallel posting
entry.content:
Hi all,

this error occurs everytime two users are modifying (whatever trcode) the same material in the same plant causing MARC block. 

So try this, run SM12 with: 

lock argument = *material*
User = USER 

SAP will show all the locks exsisting on the material. Delete all the locks and try again.

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

Thank you for your reply, so all the BTPs you listed are activate joule for sap pce mandatory required services?

entry.title: Re: FioriElements - Grouping of table rows
entry.content:
Hi Willem, thanks so much for the effort...

So I changed the Facet-Annotation from the LineItem to the PresentationVariant:
<PropertyValue Property="Target" AnnotationPath="to_RELS4BP/@UI.PresentationVariant#RELS_presentationVariant"/>.

Result ist, that the table is not displayd anymore... 😞

I tried to annotate the LineItem and PresentationVariant in CDS and in XML.... Same thing...

entry.title: Re: Enter count in MI04, have error Message no. M3897
entry.content:
Hi all,
this error occurs everytime two users are modifying (whatever trcode) the same material in the same plant causing MARC block.
So try this, run SM12 with:
lock argument = *material*
User = USER
SAP will show all the locks exsisting on the material. Delete all the locks and try again.

entry.title: Re: Business Rule : How can I round the amount into integ...
entry.content:
Hi @Napat_Iamlaor 

I think your requirement will be possible with look up table and business rule.

1. Create a look up in object definition to define the Minimum Range and Maximum Rage then round to defined number.

                 Example:- Minimum Range:- 101,629.40

                                  Maximum Range:- 101,632.35

                                  Round to:- 101,630.00 (Defined number)

2. Create business rule using Look up function to validate the salary amount and set the condition to round as per the look up value definition.

Note:- You have define the look up logic for all the number range.

 

Thanks & Regards

Arun

entry.title: Re: SDK - Create Sales Order and send for approval
entry.content:
To send sales quotations for approval in SAP Business One, follow these steps:

  1. Create a Sales Quotation: Start by creating a sales quotation in SAP Business One. Enter the relevant customer and item information.

  2. Approval Template: Set up an approval template if you haven't already. Go to the "Administration" menu, select "Approval Procedures," and then "Approval Templates." Define the conditions under which the sales quotation needs approval.

  3. Assign Approval Stages: Assign the approval stages to the relevant users. This can be done in the "Approval Stages" section under "Approval Procedures."

  4. Submit for Approval: Once the sales quotation is ready, submit it for approval. This can be done by clicking the "Add" button. The system will automatically route the quotation to the designated approvers based on the approval template.

  5. Approval Process: The approvers will receive a notification and can review the sales quotation. They can approve or reject it and add comments if necessary.

  6. Final Approval: Once all approvers have approved the sales quotation, it will be marked as approved in the system, and you can proceed with further processing.

entry.title: Re: "This message is from an external sender" creates folder in clients mail box
entry.content: You as the sender won't be able to influence what happens on the receiver end directly. The only thing you can do is check SOST to see if the generated mail looks normal.

entry.title: Re: SAPFM06P - BADI_MM_PO_OC_EMAIL for S4CORE 107 and SAPSCRIPT
entry.content: Might be time to start thinking about Adobe because it's WYSIWYG vs. SAPScript which clearly SAP has no intention of supporting in the future and is effectively like doing web development by writing HTML by hand.

entry.title: Re: SAPFM06P - BADI_MM_PO_OC_EMAIL for S4CORE 107 and SAP...
entry.content: You could also set a breakpoint at the start of FM ADDR_GET_NEXT_COMM_TYPE or CONVERT_COMM_TYPE_DATA to find an implicit improvement spot?

entry.title: SAC - Live Data Model- Content Network Storage transport
entry.content:
Hi SAC experts,

I have a HANA Cloud Live Data Model in my dev tenant and need to transport to the other tenants keeping the model format settings.

When I transport via Content Network Storage some model information's are lost, like (SAC Model - Dimensions image):

  • Dimension descriptions added at SAC Model level
  • Hidden Dimensions at SAC Model level

 

 

But for the measures I still can see the same hidden configurations (SAC Model - Measures image).

 

For, the import options I select "Overwrite objects and data".

Do you know if is SAC limitation or am I doing this wrongly?

Thank you in advance,

Rui

entry.title: Re: Restriction of Timesheet changes after approval from...

If so, how do you search for them?
Best regards
Manuel

entry.content: that's quite common, you need to send csrf token for post call.

entry.title: Re: SAP standard master roles which contain all transactions module-related

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Course assigned to a Curricula is not showing to the...
entry.content: The course was completed for the user.

entry.title: MD51 not work for project by MRP area
entry.content:
Hi Guys: We use MD51 for project planning scenrio, it works without MRP area, but if we activate MRP area, the requirement doesn't work in MD51.

Is there any addtional action needed if we use MRP area for project MRP?

Thanks.

 

Stephen

entry.title: Re: "This message is from an external sender" creates folder in clients mail box
entry.content:
Basically, their mail system creates a warning message and attaches your original message to it.

Ask the recipient to check with his mail admin, you cannot change this behaviour, but they may be able to add your sender address to some whitelist?

entry.title: Re: Recurring Schedule of Performance Forms for New hires
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content:
Hi Junwu,

I use the same API URL to get the csrf token:

odata_url = "https://****************************.abap.us10.hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zapi_tsensordat_giv_o4/srvd_a2x/sap/zapi_tsensordat_giv_o4/0001/?sap-client=100"
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}",
    "X-CSRF-Token": "Fetch"
}
csrf_response = requests.get(url=odata_url, headers=headers)
csrf_token = csrf_response.headers.get("X-CSRF-Token")

 I receive it and push it to the POST request:

odata_url = "https://******************.abap.us10.hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zapi_tsensordat_giv_o4/srvd_a2x/sap/zapi_tsensordat_giv_o4/0001/ZC_TSENSORDAT_GIV"
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "Accept": "application/json",
    "X-CSRF-Token": csrf_token
}

but receive an error  403 {"error":{"code":"/IWBEP/CM_V4H_RUN/042","message":"CSRF token is invalid","@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory":"CSRF_Token_Missing"}}

entry.title: How to Clear the 'Task' Field for New 'Effort' Creation
entry.content:
Symptom
In the Windows mobile app, the 'Task' field on the 'Effort' screen is automatically pre-filled based on previous selections, potentially resulting in incorrect time effort entries. This behavior is inconsistent with other mobile platforms, such as iOS and Android, where users must manually select the 'Task' field, aligning expectations across all devices.

Cause
This has been implemented to make the user process easy by reducing the number of steps.

Resolution
Enter 'null' in the 'Task' field within the screen configuration to clear the field for any new 'Effort' creation.

See Also
Announcement for Windows Mobile App 'Update of TimeTasks and match code behaviour (Preview Program) - 24.08.0': https://help.sap.com/whats-new/sap-field-service-mobile?Version=24.08.0&locale=en-US

entry.title: Need Assistance – Auto Unrelease PO on Any Change
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

My requirement is that if any changes are made to a released Purchase Order (PO), it should be automatically unreleased. This should apply to all types of changes, including modifications to the header text and line-item text.

I have identified the following USER EXIT for this requirement. Could you please confirm whether it will work or suggest an alternative if needed?

 Use User Exit (EXIT_SAPMM06E_013)

  1. Inside this enhancement, use the exit: EXIT_SAPMM06E_013
    • If the PO is released by looking at im_ekko-frgke. If the status equals 'G', it indicates the PO is released
    • If header text is modified (EKKO- TDLINE).
    • If change is detected (i.e. the texts differ), reset the release flag by setting EKKO-FRGKE to 'R' (or any value that represents an unreleased state).
    • Change should automatically “unrelease” the PO at all levels
  2. How to find the TDLINE main point. So that I below mentioned the explain & use the function module for read the text.
    • ID=F01
    • LANGUAGE=EN
    • NAME= 410000102400010  (PO NUMBER & ADD 00010)
    • OBJECT NAME=EKPO

 

 

                        

    If TDLINE can be changed the po automatically unrelease all levels.

The new header text (new_txt, assumed to be provided in im_ekko-tdline) is compared with the fetched text (old_txt).

MM (Materials Management) PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM) MAN Material Requirements Planning

entry.title: Reporting on Learning Attachments
entry.content: Im looking for guidance or tips on the best way to handle Attachments in Learning. e.g. I know we can upload a learners certification to an event and in the community the report posted by @Lisa bopst is very useful to see attachments at event level.  Do you know if there is a way of reporting on attachments on item level at all? we dont want to create bookable events for every item but do want to upload learning history with attachments on learners. Then we need to report on this to see who has what attachment at a later stage. Its for compliance reasons so therefore very important attachments are trackable - any suggestions most welcome. Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP standard master roles which contain all transacti...
entry.content: Yes, something like that would be what I prefer if there were roles that were module-specific, i.e. a role for CO that contains all CO transactions. Or at profile level.

entry.title: Re: Request to add a column in Tax Reconciliation Account...
entry.content:
Dear @omer_k

Thanks very much for your question and your patience. 

What sort of supplier details would you like displayed? There are quite a few possiblities within that category. 

I've consulted with the development team, and no such development is currently planned. 

One option would be to create a formal request via the Customer Influence Portal.

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: [EWM] Picking from bulk storage - Pick Point
entry.content:
Dear all, @former_member193471 ; @saravanakumardurairajan 

I'm really wondering how this should work. You said that using the A = product task partial quantity in the LOSC customizing did the trick? However, it can't work as in the Fuba /SCWM/ROUTING_LAYOUT_CHECK, one of the three criteria must be fullfilled in order for the Fuba to even check the routing information in Customizing:

HOMVE = X
or HUTO = X
or Pickpoint activated in storage type.

The first are not set because of removing from bulk storage, it's a product warehouse task. Pickpoint activated also doesn't work as the level of availability on the storage type is bin. Therefore I'm really wondering, how you get the layout control running for bulk storage in this case?

Also, even if you get to work that SAP thinks it is a picking point process, what should the initial warehouse task from the Pick storage type to Pick-point look like? Usually, SAP would remove a full HU to the pick-point. As there is no HU in the available stock, how should SAP know which quantity to use in the WT? From the packaging specification?

Thanks

Stefan

entry.title: Re: Link relationship between a purchase invoice and a PO...
entry.content: Hi Ankit, Thanks for your response. We haven't tested it yet as we're waiting for a new invoice to be created in the test system. Once that's done, we'll proceed with linking GRPO and update you accordingly. Best regards, Urvish Patel

entry.title: Re: SAP Data Quality Management (SAP DQM) and Cloud for Customer (C4C)
entry.content: Hi Alan, how did you solve to pass configuration name from C/4 to DQM?

entry.title: Re: graduation project : SAP Based Smart Job Tracking System Hello,
entry.content:
Yes! Data protection is about personal data, the simple and intuitive things you know like e-mail, Name, birthday, ... But also indirect data like geo-location tracking or work tracking.
Geo-location describes my behavior if you have my location track over months, where you see, someone leaving from street x1 in town y1 at 6 am stopping at street x2 at town y2 to arriving street x3 at town y3 at 6:30 am and same for going back. That's the guy living in town y with a kid, going to kindergarten and working at IBM. Not too many!
Work tracking is similar and fits to your project. Depending on who has access to the task list and when was what done, managers or others can derive a profile of your work attitude which is forbidden in certain countries.
Yes, I recommend to synch with DPP.

entry.title: Restricting Duplicate Notifications for Same Equipment
entry.content:
If a notification is created, first check whether a previous notification exists for the same equipment. If an open notification already exists, restrict the creation of a new one notification.

To implement this logic, I have tried using a user exit with the procedure mentioned below. Can you suggest whether this logic will work smoothly?"

User Exit and Logic for Restriction

In the QQMA0002 & QQMA0026   user exit you will use the VIQMEL table to query existing notifications and restrict new ones based on equipment.

User Exit: (QQMA0002 & QQMA0026):

Steps to Implement the Restriction Logic:

  1. Query the VIQMEL table
    • Use VIQMEL-EQUNR (Equipment Number) to find existing notifications.
    • Filter by QMART (Notification Type) is necessary.
    • Retrieve OBJNR (Object Number) from the result.
  2. Check Status in JEST Table
    • Use OBJNR to fetch the status from JEST table.
    • Filter with INACT field:
      • If INACT is blank (Active) → Restrict notification creation.
      • If INACT is 'X' (Inactive) → Allow creation.
  3. Prevent Notification Creation
    • If restriction applies, use MESSAGE statement to show an error message.

                                                     ⬇️ 

             Already have created same equipment Notification

 

Logic Description (Step-by-Step):

  1. Find Existing 'M2' Notifications: Retrieve all notifications from the VIQMEL table where EQUNR matches the equipment number and QMART is 'M2'.
  2. Check Status (JEST): For each notification found in step 1, retrieve the corresponding status from the JEST table using the OBJNR.
  3. Determine Activity: For each JEST entry:
    • If INACT is blank (space), the notification is considered active.
    • If INACT is 'X', the notification is considered inactive.
  4. Apply Restriction:
    • If at least one active 'M2' notification is found in step 3, prevent the creation of the new 'M2' notification and display an error message.
    • If all existing 'M2' notifications are inactive (INACT = 'X') or no 'M2' notifications exist (step 1 returned no results), allow the creation of the new 'M2' notification.

PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM) SAP Asset Performance Management MM (Materials Management)

entry.title: CORS -allow only specific clients -Sender rest adapter
entry.content:
Dear Team,
Greetings of the day!!!

Flow : Source -PI-SAP ECC
Need to authenticate the PO end point using(No auth)

Getting 200 OK If I maintain allow all clients - CORS.

Need to restrict for specific client.(Allow only specific clients).
As shown in image.


While checking with specific URL. getting the socket issue as
Error: tunneling socket could not be established, cause=read ECONNRESET.

Let me know if any information required
Provide me the possible way.
Note:Need to call the PI URL without credentials.
Suggest me how to proceed further .
I followed this link for CORS.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/cors-for-sap-po-rest-sender-adapter/ba-p/13415174

1.certificate based authentication
2. CORS 

Regards,
saikiran kumar.

entry.title: Re: How can I remove bullets from the creation, search an...
entry.content: Thank you very much for your help! I applied the notes and it worked correctly!

entry.title: Unlock the Future of Customer Support at SAP’s Customer Support Summit – Register Now!
entry.content:
Join us on Thursday, March 27, 2025, at SAP’s Newtown Square office for an exclusive Customer Support Summit—an event designed to inspire, inform, and connect SAP customers and partners with the latest innovations in customer support and cloud lifecycle management.

Why Attend?

Customer support is evolving rapidly, and SAP is leading the way with cutting-edge AI-powered solutions, enhanced self-service tools, and strategic support frameworks. At this summit, you'll gain insights into how SAP’s latest advancements can help you optimize operations, enhance system performance, and drive business success.

What’s in Store for You?

  • Keynote by Mohammed Ajouz – Get inspired by SAP’s Senior Vice President, Global Head of Technical Support, as he shares the vision for SAP Customer Support & Cloud Lifecycle Management.

  • Thought-Provoking Panel Discussion – Hear from SAP leaders, customers, and partners on the future of customer support, AI-driven automation, and the next wave of agentic support solutions.

  • Tech Talks & Demo Pods – Experience hands-on demonstrations of SAP’s self-service tools, AI-powered support solutions, and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

  • Networking Reception – Connect with fellow professionals, industry experts, and SAP executives in an engaging and interactive setting.

Who Should Attend?

This free-of-charge event is tailored for SAP customers and partners who want to stay ahead in the ever-evolving support landscape. Whether you’re exploring RISE with SAP, GROW with SAP, or looking for ways to optimize your support strategy, this summit offers invaluable insights and direct access to SAP experts.

Don’t miss this opportunity to engage, learn, and connect with the best in the industry.

Secure your spot today – Register now!

entry.title: Re: How to remove the delete option from Personal Data Co...
entry.content: Yes, this rule will definitely work if you wanted to avoid Onboardee submit the PDC step without those sections(Address, Biographical Info etc.). You must make sure to use the rule context just for Onboarding just to skip this rule execution during the other scenarios like Hire/rehire etc. Good luck!

entry.title: Re: Need Assistance – Auto Unrelease PO on Any Change
entry.content:
First check your current customizing - SPRO, Materials Management, Purchasing, Purchase Order, Release Procedure for Purchase Orders - Define Release Procedure for Purchase Orders - What is the value of 'Changeability of Purchasing Document During\/After Release'?

Then a BAdI like ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST (click on the link to discofer your question is a FAQ) should provide more options than those old customer exits.

Suggestion: in PROCESS_ITEM or HEADER, use the methods of the interface IF_LONGTEXTS_MM to access long texts.

entry.title: Re: VAR case forwarding via SAP Cloud ALM ITSM API
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Enter count in MI04, have error Message no. M3897
entry.content: Better ask any user to leave the transaction, when no answer for some minutes don't remove lock wih SM12 but cancel session thru SM50.

entry.title: Re: BTP ABAP Environment Fiori Launchpad Missing Administ...
entry.content:
Hi msadik,

in the SAP BTP ABAP Environment Trial systems your journey ends in the Fiori Elements Preview. You are not authorized to manage any IAM content.

Please check Get started with SAP BTP ABAP Environment: Trial Account vs. Free Tier Option for more details and differences.

best regards,
 Ingo

entry.title: SAP Integration suite
entry.content:
Hi community, is there any way to connect SAP integration cloud tenant with the servers(web socket supported) through websocket communication ?

Regards,

Mohan Sai

entry.title: CDS for Public Cloud with material, serial numbers, and storage location details
entry.content:
Hi All , 

I need a CDS view to retrieve the serial numbers and current storage location information of the materials in stock for Public Cloud. 

I couldn't find such a CDS. Could you assist me with this?

Thanks,

entry.title: Intrastat not showing automatic CN
entry.content:
#HANA #Intrastat
We are issuing billings to our EU customers. At the same minute/ hour we issue also automatic credit note which is CN correction for price (rebate) - there is no returns only price correction. The problem is that those CN are not available in Intrastat dispatches nor in Intrastat Receipts. for this we use "value cr. rd. AutoCN" request.
I've read available documentation and I know what for CN/ DN if they are related to goods they will be available in Intrastat receipts. But my case is different and I am not sure how to see CN in Intrastat

entry.title: Error "Serial number already exists for material" when creating new equipment
entry.content:
Hello, 

We are working on a scenario where fresh material is received with an external serial number successfully in stock. 

Then I'm trying to create a new equipment and assign the received material with the external number to it, but it returns an error - "Serial number already exists for material" 

Is there a way to assign already received material with serial number to new equipment?

entry.title: Re: How To Enable Export > Spreadsheet in S_ALR_87012277
entry.content:
Hello Kamal, 

You can export this data as below.

Please go to Menu >  System> List> Save > Local File .

Best Regards,

Ravindra

entry.title: Re: Cost Centers and Distribution Cycle with SKF
entry.content: Dear expert,

entry.title: Re: Skip bin section RF scan from storage bin field in EWM
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Finally to solve issue. Just needed to inform the main bin in this field in storage bin master data

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration suite
entry.content: with the servers... what servers? Web socket communication for what purpose?

entry.title: Re: Scrolling table control in module pool goes to next tab
entry.content: Solution - go to config table V_T133D (SM30) and populate checkbox field SLBKZ (Indicator: Process same screen again) for the record containing the "Local Screen" field value, "Successor Screen" value, and the function code in question.  Apparently SAP uses the record containing local screen, successor screen, and FCODE value to control auto-navigation.

entry.title: Installation of equipment to functional location via maintenance order possibility
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have a request if there is a possibility to perform an installation of equipment to functional location via corrective maintenance order? 

There is an equipment created with assigned serialized material and successful installation can be performed with IE4N or the respective Fiori app, however i`m trying to perform it via corrective maintenance order and looking for relevant information for it. 

 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content: you can see the msg is different, this time it says the token is invalid. did you debug and see what you have for csrf_token?

entry.title: subcontracting BOM components in PO do not generate requirements
entry.content:
hello all

my scenario is the following:

I created a PO with several items, one of which is a subcontracting configurable item with its BOM.

configuration is correct and the list of components of the BOM appear in the material tab.

my problem is the following:

in MD04 I can see the subcontracting line for the components but even if the system sees that the component is needed, it does not generate any requirement such as Preq or planned order.

in attachment an example: I can see the subcontracting line, there is no available stock but the requirement is not generated
